findingDetails.claim: The /prompt-improve Q2=B branch creates specs/[NNN]-[topic]/prompts/ from prompt topic text without a documented slug, containment, or shell-argument guard.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Searched the command and spec packet for slug/sanitize/containment wording around topic, new spec folder, filename, custom path, and save path; found only filename hyphen-case guidance and the previously recorded custom-path containment finding for Q2=C, not a guard for Q2=B.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The runtime operator may manually normalize [topic] before creating the folder, but the command contract does not require that normalization and is intended to be executable from the workflow text.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade or resolve if the command explicitly derives a safe slug for [topic], rejects path separators/shell metacharacters, resolves under the intended specs root, and refuses overwrite/collision cases before directory creation.

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: multi-file spec/skill/command/agent review with mutating command path handling and prior active findings

targetSelection.selectionReason: security second pass centered on mutating prompt save branches, resource path guards, and agent execution boundaries while avoiding duplicate prior findings

searchLedger.hypothesis: The new-spec-folder save branch uses user topic text as a shell-created path segment without a documented slug or containment guard.

searchActions.result: Q0 accepts topic text and Q2=B derives save_path through specs/[NNN]-[topic]/.
Q2=B creates specs/[NNN]-[topic]/prompts/ with mkdir -p; only filename generation has hyphen-case wording.
No topic slug/containment guard found; prior Q2=C custom-path issue is separate.

searchLedger.rationale: The branch presents a mutating command contract that uses topic text as a path segment without requiring safe derivation.

searchLedger.invariant: Prompt-improver agents must not execute returned prompt content or invoke tools/commands from caller-supplied text.

searchActions.result: Both runtime agents remain read-only/no-execution surfaces.

searchLedger.rationale: Agent prompt-injection-to-execution class remains blocked by read-only/no-execution contracts.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: verified by direct reads of both runtime agent definitions

searchLedger.invariant: Skill router resources must resolve under the skill root and accept only intended markdown/json resources.

searchActions.result: Hub and packet resource loaders resolve relative to SKILL_ROOT and guard resource suffix/root.

searchLedger.rationale: Resource path traversal was already guarded and no new resource escape was found.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: verified by direct read of router/resource guard pseudocode

searchLedger.invariant: Only prompt-improve should mutate workspace; prompt-models must stay read-only.

searchActions.result: prompt-improve is the only mutating workflow; prompt-models remains mutatesWorkspace:false.

searchLedger.rationale: No additional read-only packet mutation class was found in this security pass.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: verified by direct read of registry tool surfaces

title: New spec-folder save branch derives a shell-created path from unsanitized topic text

detail: security pass reused exact-search and direct-read fallback because code graph freshness was stale; graph structural relationships blocked, not treated as empty.

reason: mutating Q2=B command path lacks documented topic slug/containment guard while router resource guards pass
Both runtime prompt-improver agents remain read-only/no-execution surfaces.
Hub and packet resource loaders resolve under SKILL_ROOT and guard routable resources.
Registry keeps prompt-models read-only; no additional read-only packet mutation class found.
